428:         result: { summary: action.summary, stepsExecuted: step - 1 },

468: export async function processWorkspaceTask(env: Env, workspaceId: string, taskId: string): Promise<void> {

471:   if (!enabled) {
472:     return;

36:   if (normalized === 'false' || normalized === '0' || normalized === 'no' || normalized === 'off') {
37:     return false;
38:   }
39: 
40:   return fallback;
41: }

43: function parseInteger(
44:   value: string | undefined,
45:   fallback: number,
46:   options?: { min?: number; max?: number }
47: ): number {
48:   if (value === undefined) {
49:     return fallback;
50:   }
51: 
52:   const parsed = Number.parseInt(value.trim(), 10);
53:   if (Number.isNaN(parsed)) {
54:     return fallback;
55:   }
56: 
57:   if (options?.min !== undefined && parsed < options.min) {
58:     return options.min;
59:   }
60: 
61:   if (options?.max !== undefined && parsed > options.max) {
62:     return options.max;
63:   }
64: 
65:   return parsed;
66: }

68: function normalizeRuntimeFlags(flags: RuntimeFlags): RuntimeFlags {
69:   return {
70:     ...flags,
71:     totalTimeoutMs: Math.max(flags.totalTimeoutMs, flags.attemptTimeoutMs),
72:   };
73: }

75: export function getRuntimeFlagsFromEnv(env: Env): RuntimeFlags {
76:   const resolved: RuntimeFlags = {
77:     v2Enabled: parseBoolean(env.V2_ENABLED, DEFAULT_RUNTIME_FLAGS.v2Enabled),
78:     v2CodeBrowserEnabled: parseBoolean(

80:       DEFAULT_RUNTIME_FLAGS.v2CodeBrowserEnabled
81:     ),
82:     maxAttempts: parseInteger(env.MAX_ATTEMPTS, DEFAULT_RUNTIME_FLAGS.maxAttempts, { min: 1, max: 10 }),
83:     attemptTimeoutMs: parseInteger(

87:     ),
88:     totalTimeoutMs: parseInteger(env.TOTAL_TIMEOUT_MS, DEFAULT_RUNTIME_FLAGS.totalTimeoutMs, {
89:       min: 60000,
90:     }),

94:       { min: 1, max: 168 }
95:     ),
96:     maxRepairCycles: parseInteger(
97:       env.MAX_REPAIR_CYCLES,
98:       DEFAULT_RUNTIME_FLAGS.maxRepairCycles,
99:       { min: 0, max: 10 }
100:     ),
101:     lintBlocking: parseBoolean(env.LINT_BLOCKING, DEFAULT_RUNTIME_FLAGS.lintBlocking),
102:     testBlocking: parseBoolean(env.TEST_BLOCKING, DEFAULT_RUNTIME_FLAGS.testBlocking),
103:     safeInstallIgnoreScripts: parseBoolean(
104:       env.SAFE_INSTALL_IGNORE_SCRIPTS,

106:     ),
107:     autoInstallScriptsFallback: parseBoolean(
108:       env.AUTO_INSTALL_SCRIPTS_FALLBACK,

110:     ),
111:     rawRetentionDays: parseInteger(
112:       env.RAW_RETENTION_DAYS,

115:     ),
116:     summaryRetentionDays: parseInteger(

121:     workspaceAgentRuntimeEnabled: parseBoolean(

130: export function mergeRuntimeFlagOverrides(
131:   base: RuntimeFlags,
132:   rows: RuntimeFlagRow[]
133: ): RuntimeFlags {
134:   const merged: RuntimeFlags = { ...base };
135: 
136:   for (const row of rows) {
137:     switch (row.key) {
138:       case 'v2_enabled':
139:         merged.v2Enabled = parseBoolean(row.value, merged.v2Enabled);
140:         break;
141:       case 'v2_code_browser_enabled':
142:         merged.v2CodeBrowserEnabled = parseBoolean(row.value, merged.v2CodeBrowserEnabled);
143:         break;
144:       case 'max_attempts':
145:         merged.maxAttempts = parseInteger(row.value, merged.maxAttempts, { min: 1, max: 10 });
146:         break;

148:         merged.attemptTimeoutMs = parseInteger(row.value, merged.attemptTimeoutMs, { min: 60000 });
149:         break;

152:         break;
153:       case 'idempotency_ttl_hours':
154:         merged.idempotencyTtlHours = parseInteger(row.value, merged.idempotencyTtlHours, {
155:           min: 1,
156:           max: 168,
157:         });
158:         break;
159:       case 'max_repair_cycles':
160:         merged.maxRepairCycles = parseInteger(row.value, merged.maxRepairCycles, {
161:           min: 0,

163:         });

165:       case 'lint_blocking':

167:         break;

169:         merged.testBlocking = parseBoolean(row.value, merged.testBlocking);
170:         break;
171:       case 'safe_install_ignore_scripts':
172:         merged.safeInstallIgnoreScripts = parseBoolean(row.value, merged.safeInstallIgnoreScripts);
173:         break;
174:       case 'auto_install_scripts_fallback':
175:         merged.autoInstallScriptsFallback = parseBoolean(
176:           row.value,
177:           merged.autoInstallScriptsFallback
178:         );
179:         break;
180:       case 'raw_retention_days':
181:         merged.rawRetentionDays = parseInteger(row.value, merged.rawRetentionDays, { min: 1 });
182:         break;
183:       case 'summary_retention_days':
184:         merged.summaryRetentionDays = parseInteger(row.value, merged.summaryRetentionDays, { min: 1 });
185:         break;
186:       case 'workspace_agent_runtime_enabled':
187:         merged.workspaceAgentRuntimeEnabled = parseBoolean(row.value, merged.workspaceAgentRuntimeEnabled);
188:         break;
189:       default:
190:         break;
191:     }
192:   }
193: 
194:   return normalizeRuntimeFlags(merged);
195: }
196: 
197: export async function loadRuntimeFlags(env: Env): Promise<RuntimeFlags> {
198:   const fromEnv = getRuntimeFlagsFromEnv(env);
199: 
200:   try {
201:     const result = await env.DB
202:       .prepare('SELECT key, value FROM runtime_flags')
203:       .all<RuntimeFlagRow>();
204: 
205:     return mergeRuntimeFlagOverrides(fromEnv, result.results);
206:   } catch (error) {
207:     const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
208:     console.warn(`[flags] Failed to load runtime overrides, using env defaults: ${message}`);
209:     return fromEnv;
210:   }

parts.text:
Found two issues worth fixing before merge:

- **High – cancel + transient failure can leave tasks stuck in non-terminal `queued`**
  - In `processWorkspaceTask`, retry scheduling does not check whether cancel was already requested (`packages/worker/src/lib/workspace-task-runner.ts:517`).
  - If a running task is canceled mid-step, then hits a transient error, the catch path can set it back to `queued` with `retry_scheduled` instead of `cancelled`.
  - That retried task may never run again because claiming requires `cancel_requested_at IS NULL` (`packages/worker/src/lib/db.ts:1496`), so it can sit indefinitely as queued-but-not-runnable.
  - Suggested fix: before scheduling retry, re-read task and if `cancelRequestedAt` is set, transition to `cancelled` + emit `task_cancelled`.

- **Medium – configured task tool policy is persisted but not actually enforced**
  - API writes a per-task policy (`packages/worker/src/api/workspace-tasks.ts:219`) into `tool_policy_json` (`packages/worker/src/lib/db.ts` insert block for `workspace_tasks`).
  - Runner enforcement is hardcoded (`packages/worker/src/lib/workspace-task-runner.ts:254` and `packages/worker/src/lib/workspace-task-runner.ts:259`) and does not read task policy, so task-level guardrails are effectively ignored.
  - This also creates mismatch with declared defaults (e.g. default list in `packages/worker/src/api/workspace-tasks.ts:138` omits `git checkout`, but runtime currently permits `git checkout -- ...`).
  - Suggested fix: parse/use `tool_policy_json` during execution and enforce allow/deny/limits from that source of truth.

I couldn’t run the suite locally because the environment is on Node `v14.16.0` while this package requires `>=20`.
